volumeTitle: Children of Heracles. Hippolytus. Andromache. Hecuba
label: Hippolytus (Section 2.760 - Section 900)

sections.label: Section 2.760
sections.original: δήσαντο πλεκτὰς πεισμάτων
sections.translation: and the crew made fast its twisted cable-ends upon the beach of Munychus , and on the land stept out .

sections.label: Section 2.764
sections.original: ἀνθ ’ ὧν , οὐχ ὁσίων ἐρώ-
sections.translation: Whence comes it that her heart

sections.label: Section 2.765
sections.original: των δεινᾷ φρένας Ἀφροδί-
sections.translation: is crushed , cruelly afflicted by Aphrodite with unholy love ; so she by bitter grief o ’ erwhelmed will tie a noose within her bridal bower to fit it to her fair white neck ,

sections.label: Section 2.770
sections.original: ρᾳ , δαίμονα στυγνὸν καται-
sections.translation: too modest for this hateful lot in life , prizing o ’ er all her name and fame , and striving thus to rid her soul of passion ’ s sting .

sections.label: Section 776
sections.original: ἰοὺ ἰού ·
sections.translation: Help ! [*] ho ! To the rescue all who near the palace stand ! She hath hung herself , our queen , the wife of Theseus .

sections.label: Section 778
sections.original: — φεῦ φεῦ , πέπρακται · βασιλὶς οὐκέτ ’ ἔστι δὴ
sections.translation: Woe worth the day ! the deed is done ; our royal mistress is no more , dead she hangs in the dangling noose .

sections.label: Section 780
sections.original: οὐ σπεύσετ ’; οὐκ οἴσει τις ἀμφιδέξιον
sections.translation: Haste ! [*] some one bring a two-edged knife wherewith to cut the knot about her neck !

sections.label: Section 782
sections.original: — φίλαι , τί δρῶμεν ; ἢ δοκεῖ περᾶν δόμους
sections.translation: Friends , what shall we do ? think you we should enter the house , and loose the queen from the tight-drawn noose ?

sections.label: Section 784
sections.original: — τί δ ’; οὐ πάρεισι πρόσπολοι νεανίαι ;
sections.translation: Why should we ? Are there not young servants here ?

sections.label: Section 785
sections.original: τὸ πολλὰ πράσσειν οὐκ ἐν ἀσφαλεῖ βίου .
sections.translation: To do too much is not a safe course in life .

sections.label: Section 786
sections.original: ὀρθώσατ ’ ἐκτείνοντες ἄθλιον νέκυν ·
sections.translation: Lay [*] out the hapless corpse , straighten the limbs . This was a bitter way to sit at home and keep my master ’ s house ! [ Exit Messenger .

sections.label: Section 788
sections.original: — ὄλωλεν ἡ δύστηνος , ὡς κλύω , γυνή ·
sections.translation: She is dead , poor lady , so I hear . Already are they laying out the corpse .

sections.label: Section 790
sections.original: γυναῖκες , ἴστε τίς ποτ ’ ἐν δόμοις βοή ;
sections.translation: Ladies , can ye tell me what the uproar in the palace means ? There came the sound of servants weeping bitterly to mine ear . None of my household deign to open wide the gates and give me glad welcome as a traveller from prophetic shrines .

sections.label: Section 794
sections.original: μῶν Πιτθέως τι γῆρας εἴργασται νέον ;
sections.translation: Hath aught befallen old Pittheus ?

sections.label: Section 795
sections.original: πρόσω μὲν ἤδη βίοτος , ἀλλ ’ ὅμως ἔτ ’ ἂν
sections.translation: No . Though he be well advanced in years , yet should I mourn , were he to quit this house .

sections.label: Section 797
sections.original: οὐκ ἐς γέροντας ἥδε σοι τείνει τύχη ,
sections.translation: ’ Tis not against the old , Theseus , that fate , to strike thee , aims this blow ; prepare thy sorrow for a younger corpse .

sections.label: Section 799
sections.original: οἴμοι · τέκνων μοι μή τι συλᾶται βίος ;
sections.translation: Woe is me ! is it a child ’ s life death robs me of ?

sections.label: Section 800
sections.original: ζῶσιν , θανούσης μητρὸς ὡς ἄλγιστά σοι .
sections.translation: They live ; but , cruellest news of all for thee , their mother is no more .

sections.label: Section 801
sections.original: τί φῄς ; ὄλωλεν ἄλοχος ; ἐκ τίνος τύχης ;
sections.translation: What ! my wife dead ? By what cruel mischance ?

sections.label: Section 802
sections.original: βρόχον κρεμαστὸν ἀγχόνης ἀνήψατο .
sections.translation: About her neck she tied the hangman ’ s knot .

sections.label: Section 803
sections.original: λύπῃ παχνωθεῖσ ’, ἢ ἀπὸ συμφορᾶς τινος ;
sections.translation: Had grief so chilled her blood ? or what had befallen her ?

sections.label: Section 804
sections.original: τοσοῦτον ἴσμεν · ἄρτι γὰρ κἀγὼ δόμοις ,
sections.translation: I know but this , for I am myself but now arrived at the house

sections.label: Section 805
sections.original: Θησεῦ , πάρειμι σῶν κακῶν πενθήτρια .
sections.translation: to mourn thy sorrows , O Theseus .

sections.label: Section 806
sections.original: αἰαῖ · τί δῆτα τοῖσδ ’ ἀνέστεμμαι κάρα
sections.translation: Woe is me ! why have I crowned my head with woven garlands , when misfortune greets my embassage ? Unbolt the doors , servants ,

sections.label: Section 825
sections.original: ἐκλύεθ ’ ἁρμούς , ὡς ἴδω πικρὰν θέαν
sections.translation: loose their fastenings , that I may see the piteous sight ,

sections.label: Section 810
sections.original: γυναικός , ἥ με κατθανοῦσ ’ ἀπώλεσεν .
sections.translation: my wife , whose death is death to me . [ The palace opens , disclosing the corpse .

sections.label: Section 811
sections.original: — ἰὼ ἰὼ τάλαινα μελέων κακῶν ·
sections.translation: Woe ! woe is thee for thy piteous lot ! thou hast done thyself a hurt deep enough to overthrow this family . Ah ! ah ! the daring of it ! done to death by violence and unnatural means , the desperate effort

sections.label: Section 815
sections.original: πάλαισμα μελέας .
sections.translation: of thy own poor hand ! Who cast the shadow o ’ er thy life , poor lady ?

sections.label: Section 1.817
sections.original: ὤμοι ἐγὼ πόνων · ἔπαθον , ὦ πόλις ,
sections.translation: Ah me , my cruel lot ! sorrow hath done her worst on me . O fortune , how heavily hast thou set thy foot on me and on my house ,

sections.label: Section 1.820
sections.original: κηλὶς ἄφραστος ἐξ ἀλαστόρων τινός .
sections.translation: by fiendish hands inflicting an unexpected stain ? Nay , ’ tis complete effacement of my life , making it impossible ; for I see , alas ! so wide an ocean of grief that I can never swim to shore again , nor breast the tide of this calamity .

sections.label: Section 1.826
sections.original: τίνα λόγον τάλας , τίνα τύχαν σέθεν
sections.translation: How shall I speak of thee , my poor wife , what tale of direst suffering tell ? Thou art vanished like a bird from the covert of my hand , taking one headlong leap from me to Hades ’ halls .

sections.label: Section 1.830
sections.original: αἰαῖ αἰαῖ , μέλεα μέλεα τάδε πάθη .
sections.translation: Alas , and woe ! this is a bitter , bitter sight ! This must be a judgment sent by God for the sins of an ancestor , which from some far source I am bringing on myself .

sections.label: Section 1.834
sections.original: οὐ σοὶ τάδ ’, ὦναξ , ἦλθε δὴ μόνῳ κακά ,
sections.translation: My prince , ’ tis not to thee alone such sorrows come ;

sections.label: Section 1.835
sections.original: πολλῶν μετ ’ ἄλλων δ ’ ὤλεσας κεδνὸν λέχος .
sections.translation: thou hast lost a noble wife , but so have many others .

sections.label: Section 1.836
sections.original: τὸ κατὰ γᾶς θέλω , τὸ κατὰ γᾶς κνέφας
sections.translation: Fain would I go hide me ’ neath earth ’ s blackest depth , to dwell in darkness with the dead in misery , now that I am reft of thy dear presence ! for thou hast slain me than thyself e ’ en more .

sections.label: Section 1.840
sections.original: τίνος κλύω ; πόθεν θανάσιμος τύχα ,
sections.translation: Who can tell me what caused the fatal stroke that reached thy heart , dear wife ? Will no one tell me what befell ?

sections.label: Section 1.845
sections.original: μέλεος , οἷον εἶδον ἄλγος δόμων ,
sections.translation: doth my palace all in vain give shelter to a herd of menials ? Woe , woe for thee , my wife ! sorrows past speech , past bearing , I behold within my house ; myself a ruined man , my home a solitude , my children orphans !

sections.label: Section 1.848
sections.original: ἔλιπες οὓς ἔτεκες , ἔλιπες , ὦ φίλα
sections.translation: Gone [*] and left us hast thou , fondest wife and noblest of all women

sections.label: Section 1.850
sections.original: φέγγος ἀελίου
sections.translation: ’ neath the sun ’ s bright eye or night ’ s star-lit radiance . [*]

sections.label: Section 852
sections.original: ἰὼ τάλας · ὦ τάλας · ὅσον κακὸν ἔχει δόμος .
sections.translation: Poor house , what sorrows are thy portion now ! My eyes are wet with streams of tears to see thy fate ;

sections.label: Section 855
sections.original: τὸ δ ’ ἐπὶ τῷδε πῆμα φρίσσω πάλαι .
sections.translation: but the sequel to this tragedy has long with terror filled me .

sections.label: Section 856
sections.original: ἔα ἔα ·
sections.translation: Ha ! what means this letter ? clasped in her dear hand it hath some strange tale to tell . Hath she , poor lady , as a last request , written her bidding as to my marriage and her children ?

sections.label: Section 860
sections.original: θάρσει , τάλαινα · λέκτρα γὰρ τὰ Θησέως
sections.translation: Take heart , poor ghost ; no wife henceforth shall wed thy Theseus or invade his house . Ah ! how yon seal of my dead wife stamped with her golden ring affects my sight ! Come , I will unfold the sealed packet

sections.label: Section 865
sections.original: ἴδω τί λέξαι δέλτος ἥδε μοι θέλει .
sections.translation: and read her letter ’ s message to me .

sections.label: Section 866
sections.original: — φεῦ φεῦ · τόδ ’ αὖ νεοχμὸν ἐκδοχαῖς
sections.translation: Woe unto us ! Here is yet another evil in the train by heaven sent . Looking to what has happened , I should count my lot in life no longer worth one ’ s while to gain . [*] My master ’ s house ,

sections.label: Section 870
sections.original: φεῦ φεῦ , τῶν ἐμῶν τυράννων δόμους .
sections.translation: alas ! is ruined , brought to naught , I say . [*] Spare it , O Heaven , if it may be . Hearken to my prayer , for I see , as with prophetic eye , an omen boding mischief .

sections.label: Section 874
sections.original: οἴμοι · τόδ ’ οἷον ἄλλο πρὸς κακῷ κακόν ,
sections.translation: O horror ! woe on woe !

sections.label: Section 875
sections.original: οὐ τλητὸν οὐδὲ λεκτόν . ὦ τάλας ἐγώ .
sections.translation: and still they come , too deep for words , too heavy to bear . Ah me !

sections.label: Section 876
sections.original: τί χρῆμα ; λέξον , εἴ τί μοι λόγου μέτα .
sections.translation: What is it ? speak , if I may share in it .

sections.label: Section 877
sections.original: βοᾷ βοᾷ δέλτος ἄλαστα . πᾷ φύγω
sections.translation: This letter loudly tells a hideous tale ! where can I escape my load of woe ? For I am ruined and undone , so awful are the words I find here written clear

sections.label: Section 880
sections.original: φθεγγόμενον τλάμων .
sections.translation: as if she cried them to me ; woe is me !

sections.label: Section 881
sections.original: αἰαῖ , κακῶν ἀρχηγὸν ἐκφαίνεις λόγον .
sections.translation: Alas ! thy words declare themselves the harbingers of woe .

sections.label: Section 882
sections.original: τόδε μὲν οὐκέτι στόματος ἐν πύλαις
sections.translation: I can no longer keep the cursed tale within the portal of my lips , cruel though its utterance be . Ah me !

sections.label: Section 885
sections.original: Ἱππόλυτος εὐνῆς τῆς ἐμῆς ἔτλη θιγεῖν
sections.translation: Hippolytus hath dared by brutal force to violate my honour , recking naught of Zeus , whose awful eye is over all . O father Poseidon , once didst thou promise to fulfil three prayers of mine ; answer one of these and slay my son , let him not escape this single day ,

sections.label: Section 890
sections.original: τήνδ ’, εἴπερ ἡμῖν ὤπασας σαφεῖς ἀράς .
sections.translation: if the prayers thou gavest me were indeed with issue fraught .

sections.label: Section 891
sections.original: ἄναξ , ἀπεύχου ταῦτα πρὸς θεῶν πάλιν ·
sections.translation: O king , I do conjure thee , call back that prayer ; hereafter thou wilt know thy error . Hear , I pray .

sections.label: Section 893
sections.original: οὐκ ἔστι · καὶ πρός γ ’ ἐξελῶ σφε τῆσδε γῆς ,
sections.translation: Impossible ! Moreover I will banish him from this land , and by one of two fates shall he be struck down ;

sections.label: Section 895
sections.original: ἢ γὰρ Ποσειδῶν αὐτὸν εἰς Ἅιδου δόμους
sections.translation: either Poseidon , out of respect to my prayer , will cast his dead body into the house of Hades ; or exiled from this land , a wanderer to some foreign shore , shall he eke out a life of misery .

sections.label: Section 899
sections.original: καὶ μὴν ὅδ ’ αὐτὸς παῖς σὸς ἐς καιρὸν πάρα ,
sections.translation: Lo ! where himself doth come , thy son Hippolytus , in good time ;

sections.label: Section 900
sections.original: Ἱππόλυτος · ὀργῆς δ ’ ἐξανεὶς κακῆς , ἄναξ
sections.translation: dismiss thy hurtful rage , King Theseus , and bethink thee what is best for thy family .
